2603.24636.title: DyMRL: Dynamic Multispace Representation Learning for Multimodal Event Forecasting in Knowledge Graph
2603.24636.paper_summary: Accurate representation of multimodal knowledge is crucial for event forecasting in real-world scenarios. However, existing studies have largely focused on static settings, overlooking the dynamic acquisition and fusion of multimodal knowledge. 1) At the knowledge acquisition level, how to learn time-sensitive information of different modalities, especially the dynamic structural modality. Existing dynamic learning methods are often limited to shallow structures across heterogeneous spaces or simple unispaces, making it difficult to capture deep relation-aware geometric features. 2) At the knowledge fusion level, how to learn evolving multimodal fusion features. Existing knowledge fusion methods based on static coattention struggle to capture the varying historical contributions of different modalities to future events. To this end, we propose DyMRL, a Dynamic Multispace Representation Learning approach to efficiently acquire and fuse multimodal temporal knowledge. 1) For the former issue, DyMRL integrates time-specific structural features from Euclidean, hyperbolic, and complex spaces into a relational message-passing framework to learn deep representations, reflecting human intelligences in associative thinking, high-order abstracting, and logical reasoning. Pretrained models endow DyMRL with time-sensitive visual and linguistic intelligences. 2) For the latter concern, DyMRL incorporates advanced dual fusion-evolution attention mechanisms that assign dynamic learning emphases equally to different modalities at different timestamps in a symmetric manner. To evaluate DyMRL's event forecasting performance through leveraging its learned multimodal temporal knowledge in history, we construct four multimodal temporal knowledge graph benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DyMRL outperforms state-of-the-art dynamic unimodal and static multimodal baseline methods.
2603.24636.paper_summary_zh: 準確表達多模態知識對於現實場景中的事件預測至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有研究在很大程度上集中於靜態環境，忽略了多模態知識的動態獲取和融合。<lb/>1) 在知識獲取層面，如何學習不同模態的時間敏感信息，特別是動態結構模態。<lb/>現有的動態學習方法通常僅限於異質空間中的淺層結構或簡單的單一空間，這使得捕捉深層關係感知幾何特徵變得困難。<lb/>2) 在知識融合層面，如何學習不斷演變的多模態融合特徵。<lb/>基於靜態共同注意的現有知識融合方法難以捕捉不同模態對未來事件的變化歷史貢獻。<lb/>為此，我們提出了DyMRL，一種動態多空間表示學習方法，以有效獲取和融合多模態時間知識。<lb/>1) 對於前者問題，DyMRL將來自歐幾里得、超曲面和複數空間的時間特定結構特徵整合到關係消息傳遞框架中，以學習深層表示，反映人類在聯想思維、高階抽象和邏輯推理中的智慧。<lb/>預訓練模型賦予DyMRL時間敏感的視覺和語言智慧。<lb/>2) 對於後者的關注，DyMRL納入了先進的雙重融合-演變注意機制，以對稱的方式在不同時間戳上對不同模態分配動態學習重點。<lb/>為了評估DyMRL通過利用其在歷史中學習的多模態時間知識來進行事件預測的性能，我們構建了四個多模態時間知識圖基準。<lb/>大量實驗表明，DyMRL在性能上超越了最先進的動態單模態和靜態多模態基線方法。
2603.24636.author: Feng Zhao et.al.
2603.24636.authors: Feng Zhao, Kangzheng Liu, Teng Peng, Yu Yang, Guandong Xu
